reference_to_beat.name: r8
reference_to_beat.note: The fixed reference 5920.6 is r8's replay_tape10 model_net at n_seeds=4 (the value in r8_reference.json). r8's replay BER has real seed variance (0.0084-0.0186 across seed sets); at n_seeds=6 r8 itself scores 6471 and at n_seeds=8 6632. EVERY candidate below was re-scored against r8 at the SAME n_seeds so the comparison is apples-to-apples; the model_net deltas, not the absolute numbers, are load-bearing.

channel: replay_tape10 (the faithful 5791-burn replay; replay_doom not registerable -> single-faithful-channel, same limitation as the reference itself)
scoring_caveat: score.py model_net is NECESSARY-NOT-SUFFICIENT: it cannot see burn-to-burn carrier flips (it marks the killed 6179 config GO). Every GO below is a HEDGE-GO the morning real tape adjudicates. rs_k is picked CONSERVATIVELY (derated below the model_net-implied k_max) so an under-claim is preferred to a closure failure.

recommended_ladder.build_args: P=22, rs_k=179, D2X_P22_N256_sp2 DQPSK, short frames (today's body)
recommended_ladder.risk: NONE (proven byte-exact off a real cassette; decodable by the SHIPPING m10_decode)
recommended_ladder.why: The standing record and the safety net. If every new rung fails the morning tape, this still closes. No decoder changes. This is the floor the ladder is anchored to.

recommended_ladder.build_args: P=22, rs_k=191, SAME r8 modulation; raise FRAME_BYTES so one chirp pair covers a ~2728-sym (14.5 s) body. cap 2-3k sym for dropout-resync.
recommended_ladder.risk: LOW. Same modulation as the proven anchor -> inherits its two-capture stability; the ONLY new assumption is dropout-resync robustness of a long frame (mitigated by capping the body at 2-3k sym + interleave depth >= frame). model_net only ever IMPROVES with frame length (BER is front-loaded settling that long frames amortize).
recommended_ladder.why: The near-free MULTIPLIER on the SAFEST modulation. Bulk framing lowers replay BER (0.0186 -> 0.0078) AND amortizes the 0.37 s/frame preamble (~1.7x wall-clock). Conservative rs_k=191 still closes with margin (k_max=223 at the bulk BER). A robust, modulation-unchanged step up from the anchor.

recommended_ladder.build_args: VARIANT='d': 8-DPSK (3 bits) on the 3 CSI-cleanest carriers 3375/5250/750 Hz, DQPSK (2 bits) on the other 19. 47 bits/sym.
recommended_ladder.risk: MEDIUM. The 8-DPSK extra bit lives on the 3 carriers with the most MEASURED phase margin (p90 15.2-15.8 deg vs the 22.5 deg boundary), but a single carrier flipping burn-to-burn is the residual risk score.py can't see. Reproduced EXACTLY (6774, clean BER 0).
recommended_ladder.why: The first higher-order-modulation rung; pure differential phase (no dead amplitude axis). Beats r8 on its own lever (model_net +303 vs r8 @ n6). Requires registering the 3-carrier bit allocation with the decoder (fixed by design, not side-info).

recommended_ladder.build_args: 8-DPSK on 3375/5250/750 Hz + 4 ext-band DBPSK carriers 9375/9750/10125/10500 Hz (+3 dB boost). 51 bits/sym.
recommended_ladder.risk: MEDIUM. Combines two MEDIUM-risk levers (8-DPSK carrier-flip + ext-band HF rolloff). Both are GRACEFUL: an ext carrier softening degrades to erasures (x11 rescue), an 8-DPSK flip corrupts one carrier-block (RS-friendly). Clean BER 0; beats the best single lever short-frame.
recommended_ladder.why: The 8-DPSK + ext-band stack at conventional frame length. Demonstrates the two orthogonal levers ADD (6938 > best single 6875). A solid aggressive rung that doesn't yet bet on a long frame.

recommended_ladder.build_args: 8-DPSK on 3375/5250/750 Hz + 6 ext DBPSK carriers 9375..11250 Hz (+3 dB); long single-preamble ~2-3k-sym (10-15 s) body. 53 bits/sym.
recommended_ladder.risk: HIGH (aggressive). Stacks all three levers (8-DPSK + 6 ext + bulk frame). The 11250 Hz top-of-band carrier is the first to soften if the morning deck's HF rolloff is worse than tape10's; ext failures degrade to erasures. Bulk framing's BER-drop COMPOUNDS with the higher-order modulation (the stack's long-frame BER ratio 0.50 is STEEPER than r8's 0.62) -> the stack ALSO gets lower BER at long frames. Conservative rs_k=191 (vs k_max=219) hedges the HF + flip risk.
recommended_ladder.why: THE CAMPAIGN TOP. model_net 8535 = +44% over the 5921 reference; the bulk-frame multiplier composes multiplicatively with the stacked higher-order modulation. This is the headline stretch rung the morning master should reach for.

recommended_ladder.build_args: VARIANT='e': 8-DPSK on the 7 CSI-cleanest carriers (all p90<18 deg), DQPSK elsewhere. 51 bits/sym -> gross 9562.5.
recommended_ladder.risk: MEDIUM (the HEDGE). Spreads the 8-DPSK bit over 7 carriers instead of 3, so if 1-2 of the absolute-cleanest 3 flip burn-to-burn the rung still has headroom over the record (model_net 6525 > 5921). Replay BER 0.0213. NOTE: the n8=7 STACK (with ext) over-reaches (BER jumps to 0.024) -> hedge with dapsk-only n8=7, NOT the n8=7 stack.
recommended_ladder.why: The carrier-flip insurance rung. The 3-carrier set is the single residual risk score.py cannot adjudicate; this rung trades peak rate for robustness so a couple of carrier flips don't drop it below the record. Record it ALONGSIDE the aggressive rungs.

ladder_summary: anchor r8 (5791 proven) -> r8+bulkframe (safe multiplier) -> dapsk 8-DPSK (first higher-order) -> stack 8-DPSK+ext4 (levers compound) -> stack+bulkframe TOP (8535) -> dapsk7 robustness hedge. Conservative->aggressive, every rung an EXACT buildable config with clean-channel BER 0.
